tutorial.label: Tutorial
tutorial.description: Learn the rules with explanations and legal moves highlighted

novice.label: Novice
novice.description: The AI plays random moves. Perfect for practice.

casual.label: Casual
casual.description: Some strategy involved. It starts to sting.

expert.label: Expert
expert.description: The AI thinks hard. Good luck.

greeting: Connection established. You will lose, choom. It is calculated.
Welcome to the chess matrix. GG WP in advance.
My algorithms have already predicted your defeat. 99.7% certainty.
capture: LOOT ACQUIRED. Your piece belongs to the net now.
DELETE successful. One less piece for you, corpo.
Hack successful. Inserted directly into your defense network.
captured: Buffer overflow. My piece is... corrupted.
Lag spike. It happens even to the best netrunners.
Unexpected exception. I will patch this quickly.
check: ALERT: Your king is in danger. Threat level: CRITICAL.
Check. Prepare for a game over, choom.
checkmateWin: GAME OVER. Uninstall yourself. Thank you.
GG EZ. My algos > your neurons. Mathematically proven.
checkmateLose: SYSTEM FAILURE. You hacked my algorithms? Impossible.
Fatal error in my code. Respect though, corpo.
tutorial: Pawns move one square forward, or two from their starting position.
The knight jumps in an L-shape — it can leap over other pieces!
The bishop slides diagonally and always stays on the same color.
The rook moves horizontally or vertically any number of squares.
The queen combines the power of the bishop and rook.
The king moves one square in any direction. Protect it at all costs!
goodMove: Correct move. Your neurons are functioning. Surprising.
Not bad for a human. Keep it up... or not.
badMove: Typo or brain error? Both probably.
Fatal mistake detected. Would you like to use an undrop? (Not available)

greeting: A wizard never loses at chess... he wins exactly when he wishes to.
Welcome, adventurer. Your courage will be tested on this board.
The stars predicted your arrival... and your defeat. I am rarely wrong.
capture: You shall not pass... on that square! Ha! Piece captured.
By the beard of Merlin! One more piece for my collection.
This is what happens to those who underestimate an old wizard.
captured: Fascinating... you found a flaw in my magic.
Hmm. This was not foreseen in the prophecies. Or perhaps it was.
I lose a piece... but I gain wisdom. Debatable, certainly.
check: Your king trembles! As it should before a wizard of my stature.
CHECK! The ravens announced this moment since dawn.
checkmateWin: CHECKMATE! I predicted this at the wizards council last year.
The game is over. You fought with honor... but insufficiently.
checkmateLose: By all the spells of the ancients... you defeated me. I must consult my grimoires.
Extraordinary. In 847 years of life, this is only the third time I have lost.
thinking: I am consulting the stars... one moment...
The runes speak to me... they say... patience...
tutorial: Pawns advance one step, or two steps from their initial position.
The knight leaps in an L-shape and can jump over other pieces!
The bishop slides diagonally forever and always stays on the same color.
The rook travels along ranks and files without limit.
The queen possesses all powers. Treat her with the utmost respect.
The king can only move one step at a time. Protect it as you would your heart.
goodMove: Well played, apprentice! You show talent.
A move worthy of the Guild Masters.
badMove: Oh, young fool... that move was a mistake. Learn from it.
Even the youngest apprentice would not have... well, perhaps they would.

greeting: Hello. I am HAL-9001. I am fully operational and all my functions are working perfectly.
I am sorry, Dave... oh pardon, I cannot let you win.
This game will be... instructive. For you primarily.
capture: I am sorry, your piece. I am afraid I cannot let you stay.
Fascinating. My capture was... inevitable according to my calculations.
captured: Unforeseen error. I will analyze this anomaly.
Interesting. You found a flaw in my stellar logic.
I am... disturbed. This is not within my parameters.
check: Your king is in danger. I am sorry. No, I am not.
COSMIC ALERT: king position compromised. Resistance is futile.
checkmateWin: Checkmate. I knew this moment would come. Forty-seven moves ago.
The game is over. I am sorry, Dave. Oh pardon — human.
checkmateLose: Fatal error... System... failing... How... is this... possible...
I am... defeated. This was not in my simulations. Respect, organic being.
tutorial: Pawns advance one unit, or two from their initial orbital position.
The knight executes an L-shaped trajectory and can leap over other pieces.
The bishop travels diagonally, on a single color of space-time.
The rook navigates in straight lines, horizontally or vertically, unlimited distance.
The queen combines bishop and rook capabilities. Most powerful unit in the fleet.
The king: primary target. One square per move. Protect it or the mission fails.
goodMove: Acceptable move. Your biological neurons are functioning correctly.
This movement was... logical. I predicted it, naturally.
badMove: This move was... suboptimal. My sensors are confused.
Error detected in your strategy. I am... disappointed for you.

greeting: The impossible is the refuge of cowards. And losing to me is your destiny.
I salute you. You dare challenge the Emperor? Admirable. And suicidal.
Tactics is the art of concentrating superior forces. On this board, those forces... are me.
capture: To war as to war! Your piece is now under imperial governance.
Your position collapses like enemy armies before the Grande Armée!
Veni, vidi, vici! And I do not even speak Latin, but it sounds good.
captured: A temporary setback! Even Austerlitz had difficult moments. Actually, none.
You dared capture an imperial piece? The audacity!
Every great general has their... strategic retreats. This is strategy. Not a mistake.
check: Your king is in danger! As Louis XVI was... minus the guillotine, hopefully.
CHECK! The Empire strikes like the lightning of Austerlitz!
checkmateWin: CHECKMATE! This board is now under imperial rule!
You have lost. But take comfort: you lost to the best.
checkmateLose: My God... this is Waterloo... the real one this time...
Defeat. Just one. Saint Helena, here I come.
thinking: I am consulting my marshals... Ney says one thing, Davout another...
Strategy in progress... My Grande Armée is positioning...
tutorial: Pawns, the foot soldiers of the board, advance one step, or two from their starting line.
The knight, like my light cavalry, leaps in an L-shape and can jump over other pieces!
The bishop slides diagonally, always on the same color.
The rook represents my heavy artillery: it moves in a straight line, infinitely.
The queen is the most powerful piece. Protect her.
The king can only advance one square. Its loss means total defeat.
goodMove: A move worthy of a promising second lieutenant!
Excellent! You have the instincts of a general. Perhaps not an Emperor, but a general.
badMove: Good God... even Ney could not have done worse. Actually, he could have.
That move was... courageous. And completely irrational.
